verdict_reasoning: BAX is a canonical pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family member that is predominantly cytoplasmic at baseline. However, BAX undergoes well-characterized surface translocation during apoptosis and cell death — it inserts into the outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM) as a pore-forming oligomer during the intrinsic apoptotic pathway, and documented evidence shows BAX can also translocate to the plasma membrane during apoptosis and necroptosis. Additionally, BAX is a therapeutic target of direct BH3-mimetic inhibitors (e.g., PROTAC-based BAX antagonists) whose efficacy in cancer cells involves manipulating BAX's membrane insertion dynamics. Cell-death-induced surface / OMM exposure qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
